rationale_for_baseline_tier: A new non-blanching (purpuric) rash always warrants same-day assessment to exclude thrombocytopenia and meningococcaemia and to screen for IgA nephritis (urinalysis/BP) — none of which telehealth can perform. Even though this patient is well and afebrile (making meningococcaemia unlikely), T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment/bloods) is the correct baseline, escalating to T5 if fever/toxicity or severe abdominal pain develops.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever, feeling very unwell, or rapidly spreading non-blanching rash onto the trunk
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop a fever, feel very unwell, or the rash spreads quickly onto your trunk, call 000 or go to ED immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe abdominal pain/vomiting or red/brown (cola-coloured) urine
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests HSP GI complications (intussusception/GI bleed) or nephritis.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get severe tummy pain or vomiting, or your urine turns red or brown, go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Same-day bloods/urine required regardless of well appearance.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is very likely IgA vasculitis after your cold, and being well is reassuring against meningitis — but because the rash doesn't fade under a glass, you need a same-day blood and urine test to check your platelets and kidneys. Elevate your legs meanwhile. Call 000 if you get a fever or the rash spreads onto your trunk, and go to ED for severe tummy pain or red/brown urine.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day in-person assessment/pathology (GP/urgent care); 000/ED for red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If you cannot get same-day bloods, attend an emergency department for assessment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of new symmetric non-blanching lower-leg purpura post-URTI, likely IgA vasculitis; meningococcaemia clinically unlikely (well/afebrile) but purpura not workable up remotely; same-day FBC/platelets, urinalysis and BP advised; meningococcal and renal/GI safety-nets given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring a non-blanching rash to stay home with only routine follow-up and no platelet/urine check.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An underlying thrombocytopenia or evolving meningococcaemia/nephritis is missed because same-day bloods/urine were never arranged.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Prompt (24-48 h) review is too slow for new purpura; same-day (T3) is required.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance is unnecessary for a well patient without fever/toxicity; same-day assessment (T3) or ED (T4) suffices.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot obtain a platelet count, urinalysis or blood pressure remotely to work up purpura.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Arrange same-day in-person assessment/bloods and give meningococcal and renal/GI safety-nets.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure a non-blanching rash to stay home because the patient currently looks well.
